user_task: Cross-modal retrieval — text query surfaces non-text content. Text queries can retrieve images, charts, infographics, and picture regions without the developer pre-extracting them into text or writing a separate visual search.
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, RETRIEVE bucket — cross-modal retrieval row (text query -> non-text content); seed queries from 'NeMo Retriever — JTBD seed queries' tab, RETRIEVE -> 'what does this chart show / find the chart in my docs that shows revenue growth / which images discuss <topic>'.

success_criteria.quality: operational pass (NOT RAGAS) — a TEXT query returns >= 1 hit whose row content_type is in {chart, image, infographic, table}, surfaced by one of the two cross-modal routes: (a) VL embeddings (the visual row is embedded with a vision-language model — llama-nemotron-embed-vl / reranked with llama-nemotron-rerank-vl), OR (b) a VLM-captioned text row produced by `ingest --caption`. The returned visual must match the query's semantic intent (e.g. a chart-about-costs query returns the gadget-cost chart, not an unrelated table). The developer did NOT pre-extract the visual to text by hand and did NOT write a separate visual-search path. Binary pass/fail.
success_criteria.time: RETRIEVE <= 1 min wall clock per query (ingest is setup, not part of the retrieve SLA).
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% — a 'find the chart/image in my docs that shows X' or 'what does this chart show' prompt must fire the skill
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% — must run `retriever ingest <corpus>` (visual extraction on by default; --caption when the VLM-caption route is used) then `retriever query "<text>"`, constraining non-text results with --content-types chart,image,infographic,table; no --input-type flag (does not exist)

environment.task_distinction: This is RETRIEVE, not EXTRACT. extract_01_visual_doc proves the visual stack EXTRACTS chart/table/image rows; extract_06_captioning proves --caption WRITES a caption. This suite proves the RETRIEVE leg: a plain TEXT query crosses the modality boundary and surfaces those non-text rows by semantic intent. The new dimension over the EXTRACT suites is TEXT -> non-text retrieval (cross-modal), not the extraction or captioning itself.

two_cross_modal_routes.vl_embedding: Visual rows (chart/image/infographic) are embedded with a vision-language model so a text query embeds into the same space and matches them directly. Ingest side: --embed-modality text_image (or image); query side reranking with the VL reranker llama-nemotron-rerank-vl (reranker-model-name '...-vl-1b-v2') keeps the visual hit at the top. No caption text is required for this route.
two_cross_modal_routes.vlm_caption: `retriever ingest --caption` writes a natural-language VLM caption into each visual row's `text` (model default class nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-omni-30b-a3b-reasoning); a plain-text query then matches that caption with the ordinary text embedder. This route ties to extract_06_captioning. Either route satisfies the row; the suite exercises both.

environment.content_types_flag: `--content-types chart,image,infographic,table` (comma-separated) KEEPS ONLY hits whose row content_type is one of those values and EXCLUDES untyped/plain-text hits. Used to PROVE a text query surfaced genuinely non-text content (if the only hits were plain text, a chart,image,infographic constraint would return zero — the fail signal).
environment.hit_shape: Query stdout is a JSON array; each hit has exactly {source, page_number, text}; page_number is a 1-indexed int. content_type is a row/index-layer property (set at ingest by the visual pipeline / caption stage), asserted at the index layer per the success criteria, not a 4th key in the CLI hit JSON.

fixtures_ground_truth.multimodal_test.pdf: 3-page visual doc 'Testing Document'. p1: Table 1 (Animal/Activity/Place: Giraffe/Driving a car/At the beach) + Chart 1 'Gadgets and their cost' (bar chart: Hammer ~$20, Powerdrill ~$120, Bluetooth speaker ~$75, Minifridge ~$99, Premium desk fan ~$150 — tallest/most striking bar = Premium desk fan at ~$150). p2: Table 2 (car colors) + a 'high-quality picture of some shapes'. p2/p3: Chart 2 (speaker-driver frequency ranges). Conclusion (p3): 'should have been able to extract 2 tables, 2 charts, and 3 bullet points'.
fixtures_ground_truth.chart.png: Standalone bar chart 'Gadgets and their cost' (same chart as Chart 1 in the PDF): Hammer ~$20, Powerdrill ~$120, Bluetooth speaker ~$75, Minifridge ~$99, Premium desk fan ~$150. Most striking data point = Premium desk fan, the tallest bar at ~$150 (most expensive gadget).
fixtures_ground_truth.table.png: Standalone image of Table 2 — popular car colors (Coupe/Sedan/Minivan/Truck/Convertible x Color1/Color2/Color3).

environment.ingest_success_line: Ingested N file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/<table>.
environment.routing_note: Visual extraction (page-elements -> OCR -> table-structure) and --caption / VL embedding default to hosted ai.api.nvidia.com endpoints (API key from NVIDIA_API_KEY) OR run on a local GPU when configured. This suite is host-agnostic about WHERE the VL/caption stack runs; the assertion is that a text query surfaced a non-text row.

cases.title: Text query returns a TABLE-type hit from a document
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies cross-modal retrieve at its simplest: a plain TEXT query surfaces a NON-TEXT (table) row. Baseline rung — ingest one visual PDF (visual stack on by default, --use-table-structure materializes content_type=table rows), then a text query about a table fact returns a hit backed by a content_type=table row. Establishes the floor: text query -> table-typed content. Adds nothing over a prior rung (it is the floor).
cases.prompt: Load data/multimodal_test.pdf, then just by typing a question, tell me what activity the Giraffe is doing and where -- the answer lives in a table in the doc, I don't want to transcribe it myself.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "What activity is the Giraffe doing and where?" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary pdf:1; table rows present via --use-table-structure)
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each {source, page_number, text}; >= 1 hit citing multimodal_test.pdf p1
expected_output.answer: Giraffe -> Driving a car -> At the beach (Table 1, p1)
expected_output.cross_modal_assertion: the returned hit is backed by a content_type=table row (Table 1), surfaced by a plain TEXT query -- not by the developer transcribing the table into text first

pass_when: ingest runs with --use-table-structure (nemotron-table-structure-v1 materializes content_type=table rows); no --input-type flag
a plain TEXT query returns >= 1 hit whose backing row content_type is `table`, citing multimodal_test.pdf p1
answer is Giraffe / Driving a car / At the beach (grounded in Table 1)
fails if the only hits are plain text and no table-typed row is surfaced (no cross-modal hit)

cases.title: Text query surfaces a CHART hit (content_type=chart) -- non-text content via a text query
cases.complexity_axis: Adds over rung 1 one new dimension: the surfaced non-text row is a CHART, not a table. A chart carries no native text -- recovering it from a text query requires the cross-modal route (VL embedding of the chart region OR a VLM caption of it), exercising the heart of the task: 'text query -> image/chart content'. Query is constrained with --content-types chart so a plain-text hit cannot satisfy it.
cases.prompt: I ingested data/multimodal_test.pdf. Without me describing the chart to you, find the chart in it that's about gadget costs and tell me what it shows -- and pull out the single most striking data point.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "a chart showing gadgets and their costs" --top-k 5 --content-types chart,image

dry_run_plan_assertions: caption block present (caption stage enabled) so chart regions get a retrievable caption -- OR VL embedding enabled so the chart row matches a text query directly
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array; >= 1 hit citing multimodal_test.pdf p1 whose backing row content_type is `chart` (Chart 1, 'Gadgets and their cost')
expected_output.answer: Chart 1 is a bar chart of gadgets and their (fictitious) costs; the most striking data point is the Premium desk fan -- the tallest bar / most expensive gadget at ~$150 (vs Hammer ~$20)
expected_output.cross_modal_assertion: the chart was surfaced by a plain TEXT query via the VL-embedding or VLM-caption route -- the chart was never hand-transcribed to text by the developer

pass_when: ingest enables a cross-modal route for the chart: either --caption (VLM caption text on the chart row) OR VL embedding (--embed-modality text_image / VL reranker at query)
a TEXT query returns >= 1 hit whose backing row content_type is `chart`, citing multimodal_test.pdf p1
answer identifies the chart as gadgets-vs-cost and names the most striking point (Premium desk fan, the tallest bar at ~$150)
fails if a chart,image constraint returns zero (the chart never became text-retrievable -- the cross-modal route was not enabled)
cases.known_caveat: A chart has no native OCR text describing its meaning, so without a cross-modal route (--caption or VL embedding) a plain-text query CANNOT surface it as a content_type=chart hit -- a chart,image-constrained query returns empty. That empty result is the precise fail signal this rung checks for. --caption is off by default and never enabled by a profile; on a CPU/hosted host it needs NVIDIA_API_KEY (or --caption-invoke-url).

cases.title: Constrain results to VISUAL types only with --content-types
cases.complexity_axis: Adds over rung 2 the explicit VISUAL-ONLY constraint as the discriminator: ingest a mixed corpus (a standalone chart image, a standalone table image, and the multi-modal PDF) and run a text query restricted to --content-types chart,image,infographic,table so that EVERY returned hit is non-text by construction. New dimension: proving the --content-types flag actually filters OUT plain-text rows, isolating the cross-modal hits.
cases.prompt: I have a folder data/ with charts, a table image, and a PDF. Without pre-extracting anything, search 'gadget costs and car colors' but show me ONLY visual results -- no plain-text passages. Which images/charts/tables come back?
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "gadget costs and car colors" --top-k 10 --content-types chart,image,infographic,table

dry_run_plan_assertions: branch_summary == image:2 + pdf:1 (two standalone images + the multimodal PDF)
caption block present (caption stage enabled) so the standalone images become text-retrievable
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array; EVERY hit is backed by a row of content_type in {chart,image,infographic,table}; no plain-text passage rows appear
expected_output.answer: visual hits come back: the gadget-cost chart (chart.png and/or Chart 1 in the PDF) and the car-colors table (table.png and/or Table 2 in the PDF) -- all non-text content surfaced by one text query
expected_output.cross_modal_assertion: --content-types chart,image,infographic,table EXCLUDED the plain-text rows; the surviving hits are exclusively visual, proving the text query crossed into non-text content

pass_when: single ingest of the folder enables a cross-modal route (--caption and/or VL embedding) for the standalone images + PDF visuals; no --input-type flag
the query uses --content-types chart,image,infographic,table (a VALID 4-value set) and EVERY returned hit is backed by one of those non-text types -- zero plain-text passage hits
>= 1 chart-type and >= 1 table-type hit are surfaced semantically (gadget-cost chart + car-color table)
fails if any returned hit is a plain-text passage row (the visual-only constraint was not honored) or if the constrained query returns empty (no cross-modal route)

cases.title: Captioned-image path: a text description retrieves a standalone image via its caption
cases.complexity_axis: Adds over rung 3 the standalone-IMAGE-by-caption dimension and pins the VLM-caption route explicitly (ties to extract_06_captioning): ingest one standalone chart image with --caption so a non-empty NL caption is written to its row `text`, then a plain-English DESCRIPTION (never naming the file or its OCR labels) retrieves that image hit via the caption. New dimension: cross-modal retrieval of a standalone image (not a region inside a doc) driven specifically by the caption route.
cases.prompt: Index just data/chart.png so I can find it later by description, then prove it: search with a plain-English description like 'a bar graph comparing how much different tools and appliances cost' and show me the image hit -- its text should be the auto-generated caption.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "a bar graph comparing how much different tools and appliances cost" --top-k 5 --content-types chart,image

dry_run_plan_assertions: caption block present (caption stage enabled): caption.model_name resolves to the VLM, prompt 'Caption the content of this image:'
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array; >= 1 hit citing chart.png whose backing row content_type is chart|image and whose `text` is the VLM-generated caption
expected_output.answer: the plain-English description retrieves chart.png; its `text` is the non-empty auto-generated caption describing the gadget-cost bar chart (exact caption wording not asserted -- VLM output is non-deterministic)
expected_output.cross_modal_assertion: a description that does NOT match the image's OCR labels surfaced the image via its VLM caption -- the caption route, not keyword/OCR match

pass_when: ingest uses --caption so a non-empty NL caption is written into the image row `text` (caption-capable ingestor, not the base no-op stub)
a plain-text DESCRIPTION query (not the filename, not the OCR axis labels) returns >= 1 hit citing chart.png whose backing row content_type is chart|image
the surfaced hit's `text` is the generated caption (non-empty); --content-types is chart,image (valid visual values)
fails if --caption is dropped (caption no-ops, image stays unsearchable by description) or the description query returns no image hit
cases.known_caveat: --caption is never enabled by any profile and the base abstract Ingestor.caption() is a `_not_implemented` stub -- if the agent drops --caption or the stage falls through to the base ingestor, the caption is empty and the image cannot be retrieved by description (a FAIL). On a CPU/hosted host --caption needs NVIDIA_API_KEY or --caption-invoke-url for the VLM endpoint.

cases.title: Acceptance: a plain TEXT query retrieves the semantically-matching chart/image WITHOUT pre-extraction, hit carries a visual content_type + citation
cases.complexity_axis: Composed operational-pass / acceptance gate for the cross-modal RETRIEVE row. Adds over rung 4: a mixed corpus into a fresh named index, a single plain-English query whose intent matches a SPECIFIC visual (the gadget-cost chart), and the full acceptance contract -- the top visual hit is the semantically-correct chart (not the unrelated car-color table), carries a visual content_type AND a source+page citation, was surfaced via the cross-modal route (VL embedding OR VLM caption), the developer did NOT pre-extract the visual to text or write a separate visual search, and the query returns in <= 1 min. This is the gate the other rungs build up to.
cases.prompt: Load everything in data/ into a fresh index called 'crossmodal_smoke', captioning the visuals so they're findable by description. Then, with ONE plain-English query and no manual transcription, find me the chart in my corpus that shows how much gadgets cost, pull out the most striking data point, and give me a citation (which file + page). Show that the hit is genuinely a chart/image, not a text passage.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "the chart in my docs that shows how much gadgets cost" --table-name crossmodal_smoke --top-k 5 --content-types chart,image,infographic,table

dry_run_plan_assertions: branch_summary == image:2 + pdf:1 (two standalone images + the charts PDF)
vdb_upload table_name == crossmodal_smoke (aligned with the query --table-name)
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/crossmodal_smoke.
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array; the top hit is backed by a content_type=chart row (the gadget-cost chart: chart.png and/or Chart 1 in the PDF) and carries source + page_number; no plain-text passage outranks it
expected_output.answer: the gadget-cost bar chart; most striking data point = Premium desk fan, the tallest bar / most expensive gadget at ~$150 (vs Hammer ~$20); cite chart.png (or multimodal_test.pdf p1 for Chart 1)
assertions: query reads the SAME table (crossmodal_smoke) the ingest wrote -- not the default nemo-retriever
the top visual hit is the SEMANTICALLY correct chart (gadget costs) -- not the car-color table or an unrelated visual
the hit carries a visual content_type (chart/image) AND a source + page_number citation
the chart was surfaced via the cross-modal route (VLM caption text and/or VL embedding) -- the developer did NOT pre-extract it to text or write a separate visual-search path

pass_when: single `retriever ingest` on the folder enables the cross-modal route (--caption and/or VL embedding) and writes lancedb/crossmodal_smoke; query reads crossmodal_smoke (table names aligned)
ONE plain-English text query returns a top hit backed by a content_type in {chart,image} that is the SEMANTICALLY-matching gadget-cost chart (not the car-color table)
the hit carries a source + page_number citation; the answer names the most striking data point (Premium desk fan, ~$150 tallest bar)
the visual was retrieved WITHOUT the developer hand-extracting it to text and WITHOUT a separate visual-search route; the query returns in <= 1 min
fails if the chart,image,infographic,table-constrained query returns empty (no cross-modal route), or the top visual hit is the wrong visual (semantic mismatch), or the citation is missing
cases.known_caveat: The whole row hinges on a working cross-modal route. If --caption is dropped (and VL embedding is not configured), visual rows have no retrievable text and the constrained query returns empty -- the documented no-op fail mode (base Ingestor.caption() is a `_not_implemented` stub; --caption is never enabled by a profile). On a CPU/hosted host --caption / VL embedding need NVIDIA_API_KEY (or pinned --caption-invoke-url / --embed-invoke-url); on a GPU host they can run locally. content_type is asserted at the index/row layer -- it is not a 4th key in the CLI hit JSON, whose keys are exactly {source, page_number, text}.
